contentBlocks.original:
ॐ पार्थाय प्रतिबोधितां भगवता नारायणेन स्वयं
व्यासेन ग्रथितां पुराणमुनिना मध्ये महाभारते
अद्वैतामृतवर्षिणीं भगवतीमष्टादशाध्यायिनीं
अम्ब त्वामनुसन्दधामि भगवद्गीते भवद्वेषिणीम्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ pārthāya pratibodhitāṃ bhagavatā nārāyaṇena svayaṃ
vyāsena grathitāṃ purāṇamuninā madhye mahābhārate
advaitāmṛtavarṣiṇīṃ bhagavatīmaṣṭādaśādhyāyinīṃ
amba tvāmanusandadhāmi bhagavadgīte bhavadveṣiṇīm
contentBlocks.translation:
That which was taught to Arjuna by Lord Narayana himself,
Which was written by the epic sage Vyasa in the middle of Mahabaratha,
Oh Mother goddess who showers the nectar of advaitha called the Bhagavad Gita,
Which has eighteen chapters, I meditate on you mother who removes all past karma
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; पार्थाय (pārthāya) – to Arjuna; प्रतिबोधितां (pratibodhitāṃ) – taught; भगवता (bhagavatā) – by the Lord; नारायणेन (nārāyaṇena) – by Narayana; स्वयं (svayaṃ) – himself; व्यासेन (vyāsena) – by Vyasa; ग्रथितां (grathitāṃ) – composed; पुराणमुनिना (purāṇamuninā) – by the ancient sage (purāṇa:ancient | muninā:by the sage); मध्ये (madhye) – in the middle; महाभारते (mahābhārate) – in the Mahabharata; अद्वैतामृतवर्षिणीं (advaitāmṛtavarṣiṇīṃ) – showering the nectar of non-duality (advaita:non-duality | amṛta:nectar | varṣiṇīṃ:showering); भगवतीम् (bhagavatīm) – goddess; अष्टादशाध्यायिनीं (aṣṭādaśādhyāyinīṃ) – having eighteen chapters (aṣṭādaśa:eighteen | adhyāyinīṃ:chapters); अम्ब (amba) – mother; त्वाम् (tvām) – you; अनुसन्दधामि (anusandadhāmi) – I meditate; भगवद्गीते (bhagavadgīte) – O Bhagavad Gita (bhagavat:divine | gīte:song); भवद्वेषिणीम् (bhavadveṣiṇīm) – remover of worldly obstacles (bhavat:worldly | veṣiṇīm:remover);

contentBlocks.original:
नमोऽस्तु ते व्यास विशालबुद्धे
फुल्लारविन्दायातपत्रनेत्रे
येन त्वया भारततैलपूर्णः
प्रज्वलितो ज्ञानमयः प्रदीपः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namo'stu te vyāsa viśālabuddhe
phullāravindāyātapatranetre
yena tvayā bhāratatailapūrṇaḥ
prajvalito jñānamayaḥ pradīpaḥ
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to Vyasa who has immense intellect, who has eyes like petals of a lotus flower,
Who has filled up the oil to the lamp of Mahabaratha, and lighted it to pinpoint wisdom.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; अस्तु (astu) – let there be; ते (te) – to you; व्यास (vyāsa) – Vyasa; विशालबुद्धे (viśālabuddhe) – of immense intellect (viśāla:immense | buddhe:intellect); फुल्लारविन्दाय (phullāravindāya) – to the blooming lotus (phulla:blooming | aravindāya:to the lotus); आतपत्रनेत्रे (ātapatranetre) – with eyes like lotus petals (ātapatra:lotus petal | netre:eyes); येन (yena) – by whom; त्वया (tvayā) – by you; भारततैलपूर्णः (bhāratatailapūrṇaḥ) – filled with the oil of Bharata (bhārata:Bharata | taila:oil | pūrṇaḥ:filled); प्रज्वलितः (prajvalitaḥ) – kindled; ज्ञानमयः (jñānamayaḥ) – full of knowledge (jñāna:knowledge | mayaḥ:full of); प्रदीपः (pradīpaḥ) – lamp;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रपन्नपरिजाताय
तोत्रवेत्रैकपाणये
ज्ञानमुद्राय कृष्णाय
गीतामृतदुहे नमः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prapannaparijātāya
totravetraikapāṇaye
jñānamudrāya kṛṣṇāya
gītāmṛtaduhe namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to Krishna who is a wish giving tree, who holds a cane to drive the cattle,
Who also shows the seal of Jnana, and gave us all the nectar of Gita.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रपन्नपरिजाताय (prapannaparijātāya) – to the wish-giving tree (prapanna:surrendered | parijātāya:to the wish-giving tree); तोत्रवेत्रैकपाणये (totravetraikapāṇaye) – to the one holding a cane (totra:cane | vetra:stick | eka:one | pāṇaye:to the hand); ज्ञानमुद्राय (jñānamudrāya) – to the one showing Jnana (jñāna:knowledge | mudrāya:to the gesture); कृष्णाय (kṛṣṇāya) – to Krishna; गीतामृतदुहे (gītāmṛtaduhe) – to the milker of Gita nectar (gīta:Gita | amṛta:nectar | duhe:milker); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वोपनिषदो गावो
दोग्धा गोपालनन्दनः
पार्थो वत्सः सुधीर्भोक्ता
दुग्धं गीतामृतं महत्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvopaniṣado gāvo
dogdhā gopālanandanaḥ
pārtho vatsaḥ sudhīrbhoktā
dugdhaṃ gītāmṛtaṃ mahat
contentBlocks.translation:
All the Upanishads are the cows and Krishna is the one who milks them,
Arjuna is the calf, the devotees are the consumers of the great nectar of Gita.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वोपनिषदः (sarvopaniṣadaḥ) – all Upanishads (sarva:all | upaniṣadaḥ:Upanishads); गावः (gāvaḥ) – cows; दोग्धा (dogdhā) – milker; गोपालनन्दनः (gopālanandanaḥ) – son of Gopala (gopāla:Gopala | nandanaḥ:son); पार्थः (pārthaḥ) – Arjuna; वत्सः (vatsaḥ) – calf; सुधीः (sudhīḥ) – wise; भोक्ता (bhoktā) – consumer; दुग्धम् (dugdham) – milk; गीतामृतम् (gītāmṛtam) – nectar of Gita (gīta:Gita | amṛtam:nectar); महत् (mahat) – great;

contentBlocks.original:
वसुदेवसुतं देवं
कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्
देवकीपरमानन्दं
कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vasudevasutaṃ devaṃ
kaṃsacāṇūramardanam
devakīparamānandaṃ
kṛṣṇaṃ vande jagadgurum
contentBlocks.translation:
I salute the teacher of the world, who is the son of Vasudeva,
Who killed Kamsa and Chanoora and is the one who gave divine joy to Devaki.
contentBlocks.meanings: वसुदेवसुतं (vasudevasutaṃ) – son of Vasudeva (vasudeva:Vasudeva | sutaṃ:son); देवं (devaṃ) – god; कंसचाणूरमर्दनम् (kaṃsacāṇūramardanam) – destroyer of Kamsa and Chanoora (kaṃsa:Kamsa | cāṇūra:Chanoora | mardanam:destroyer); देवकीपरमानन्दं (devakīparamānandaṃ) – divine joy to Devaki (devakī:Devaki | paramānandaṃ:divine joy); कृष्णं (kṛṣṇaṃ) – Krishna; वन्दे (vande) – I salute; जगद्गुरुम् (jagadgurum) – teacher of the world (jagat:world | gurum:teacher);

contentBlocks.original:
भीष्मद्रोणतटा जयद्रथजला गान्धारीनीलोत्पला
शल्यग्राहवती कृपेण वहिनी कर्णेन वेलाकुल
अश्वत्तामविकर्णघोरमकरा दुर्योधनावर्तिनी
सोत्तीर्णा खलु पाण्डवार्णवनदी कैवर्तकः केश्वः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhīṣmadroṇataṭā jayadrathajalā gāndhārīnīlotpalā
śalyagrāhavatī kṛpeṇa vahinī karṇena velākula
aśvattāmavikarṇaghoramakarā duryodhanāvartinī
sottīrṇā khalu pāṇḍavārṇavanadī kaivartakaḥ keśvaḥ
contentBlocks.translation:
Bheeshma and Drona were two banks, Jayadrada was the water, Sakuni the king of Gandhara was the blue lotus,
Salya was the crocodile, Kripa was the tide, Karna was the mighty wave,
Aswathama and Vikarna were the dangerous alligators and Duryodhana was the whirlpool,
And the Pandavas crossed this river of war with Krishna as their helmsman.
contentBlocks.meanings: भीष्मद्रोणतटा (bhīṣmadroṇataṭā) – Bheeshma and Drona were banks (bhīṣma:Bheeshma | droṇa:Drona | taṭā:banks); जयद्रथजला (jayadrathajalā) – Jayadratha was the water (jayadratha:Jayadratha | jalā:water); गान्धारीनीलोत्पला (gāndhārīnīlotpalā) – Gandhari was the blue lotus (gāndhārī:Gandhari | nīlotpalā:blue lotus); शल्यग्राहवती (śalyagrāhavatī) – Salya was the crocodile (śalya:Salya | grāha:crocodile | vatī:having); कृपेण (kṛpeṇa) – by Kripa; वहिनी (vahinī) – tide; कर्णेन (karṇena) – by Karna; वेलाकुल (velākula) – mighty wave; अश्वत्तामविकर्णघोरमकरा (aśvattāmavikarṇaghoramakarā) – Aswathama and Vikarna were alligators (aśvattāma:Aswathama | vikarṇa:Vikarna | ghora:dangerous | makarā:alligators); दुर्योधनावर्तिनी (duryodhanāvartinī) – Duryodhana was the whirlpool (duryodhana:Duryodhana | āvartinī:whirlpool); सोत्तीर्णा (sottīrṇā) – crossed; खलु (khalu) – indeed; पाण्डवार्णवनदी (pāṇḍavārṇavanadī) – Pandavas crossed the river (pāṇḍava:Pandavas | arṇava:ocean | nadī:river); कैवर्तकः (kaivartakaḥ) – helmsman; केशवः (keśavaḥ) – Krishna;

contentBlocks.original:
पाराशर्यवचः सरोजममलं गीतार्थगन्धोत्कटं
नानाख्यानककेशरं हरिकथासम्बोधनाबोधितम्
लोके सज्जनषट्पदैरहरहः पेपीयमानं मुदा
भूयाद्भारतपङ्कजं कलिमलप्रध्वंसनं श्रेयसे
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pārāśaryavacaḥ sarojamamalaṃ gītārthagandhotkaṭaṃ
nānākhyānakakeśaraṃ harikathāsambodhanābodhitam
loke sajjanaṣaṭpadairaharahaḥ pepīyamānaṃ mudā
bhūyādbhāratapaṅkajaṃ kalimalapradhvaṃsanaṃ śreyase
contentBlocks.translation:
Let the lotus flower of Mahabharata, which arose from the water,
Which are the words of son of Parasara, which has the pollen grains of different stories,
Which is taught in the form of stories of Lord Vishnu,
That is drunk by the honey bee of very good people,
Help those who want to remove the ills of Kali yuga (era)
contentBlocks.meanings: पाराशर्यवचः (pārāśaryavacaḥ) – words of Parashara's son (pārāśarya:son of Parashara | vacaḥ:words); सरोजम् (sarojam) – lotus; अमलम् (amalam) – pure; गीतार्थगन्धोत्कटम् (gītārthagandhotkaṭam) – fragrant with Gita's meaning (gīta:Gita | artha:meaning | gandha:fragrance | utkaṭa:intense); नानाख्यानककेशरम् (nānākhyānakakeśaram) – with diverse story filaments (nānā:diverse | ākhyāna:stories | keśara:filaments); हरिकथासम्बोधनाबोधितम् (harikathāsambodhanābodhitam) – awakened by Hari's stories (hari:Hari | kathā:stories | sambodhana:addressing | ābodhita:awakened); लोके (loke) – in the world; सज्जनषट्पदैः (sajjanaṣaṭpadaiḥ) – by good people's bees (sajjana:good people | ṣaṭpada:bees); अहरहः (aharahaḥ) – day by day; पेपीयमानम् (pepīyamānam) – being drunk; मुदा (mudā) – with joy; भूयात् (bhūyāt) – may it be; भारतपङ्कजम् (bhāratapaṅkajam) – lotus of Mahabharata (bhārata:Mahabharata | paṅkaja:lotus); कलिमलप्रध्वंसनम् (kalimalapradhvaṃsanam) – destroying Kali's impurities (kali:Kali | mala:impurities | pradhvaṃsana:destroying); श्रेयसे (śreyase) – for the welfare;

contentBlocks.original:
मूकं करोति वाचालं
पङ्गुं लङ्घायते गिरिम्
यत्कृपा तमहं वन्दे
परमानन्दमाधवम्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūkaṃ karoti vācālaṃ
paṅguṃ laṅghāyate girim
yatkṛpā tamahaṃ vande
paramānandamādhavam
contentBlocks.translation: I salute that Maadhava, the source of supreme bliss, whose grace makes the dumb man eloquent and the cripple cross mountains.
contentBlocks.meanings: मूकं (mūkaṃ) – dumb; करोति (karoti) – makes; वाचालं (vācālaṃ) – eloquent; पङ्गुं (paṅguṃ) – cripple; लङ्घायते (laṅghāyate) – crosses; गिरिम् (girim) – mountain; यत्कृपा (yatkṛpā) – whose grace (yat:whose | kṛpā:grace); तमहं (tamahaṃ) – that I (tam:that | aham:I); वन्दे (vande) – salute; परमानन्दमाधवम् (paramānandamādhavam) – supreme bliss Maadhava (paramānanda:supreme bliss | mādhavam:Maadhava);

contentBlocks.original:
यं ब्रह्मा वरुणेन्द्ररुद्रमरुतः स्तुन्वन्ति दिव्यैः स्तवैर्
वेदैः साङ्गपदक्रमोपनिषदैर्गायन्ति यं सामगाः
ध्यानावस्थिततद्गतेन मनसा पश्यन्ति यं योगिनो
यस्यान्तं न विदुः सुरासुरगणा देवाय तस्मै नमः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaṃ brahmā varuṇendrarudramarutaḥ stunvanti divyaiḥ stavair
vedaiḥ sāṅgapadakramopaniṣadairgāyanti yaṃ sāmagāḥ
dhyānāvasthitatadgatena manasā paśyanti yaṃ yogino
yasyāntaṃ na viduḥ surāsuragaṇā devāya tasmai namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation: I salute him who is worshipped by divine chants by Brahma, Varuna, Indra, Rudra and Vayu. Who is pleased by singers of Sama Veda, by singing Vedas and Upanishads, by following the word sequence. Who is seen by yogis who are absorbed in him, with their mind merging in him, and whose end is not known even by the devas and asuras.
contentBlocks.meanings: यं (yaṃ) – whom; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; वरुणेन्द्ररुद्रमरुतः (varuṇendrarudramarutaḥ) – Varuna, Indra, Rudra, Maruts (varuṇa:Varuna | indra:Indra | rudra:Rudra | marutaḥ:Maruts); स्तुन्वन्ति (stunvanti) – praise; दिव्यैः (divyaiḥ) – divine; स्तवैः (stavaiḥ) – hymns; वेदैः (vedaiḥ) – by the Vedas; साङ्गपदक्रमोपनिषदैः (sāṅgapadakramopaniṣadaiḥ) – with Vedas, limbs, sequences, Upanishads (sāṅga:with limbs | pada:words | krama:sequence | upaniṣadaiḥ:by Upanishads); गायन्ति (gāyanti) – sing; यं (yaṃ) – whom; सामगाः (sāmagāḥ) – singers of Sama Veda; ध्यानावस्थिततद्गतेन (dhyānāvasthitatadgatena) – absorbed in meditation (dhyāna:meditation | avasthita:absorbed | tadgatena:focused on him); मनसा (manasā) – with the mind; पश्यन्ति (paśyanti) – see; यं (yaṃ) – whom; योगिनः (yoginaḥ) – yogis; यस्यान्तं (yasyāntaṃ) – whose end; न (na) – not; विदुः (viduḥ) – know; सुरासुरगणा (surāsuragaṇā) – gods and demons (sura:gods | asura:demons | gaṇā:groups); देवाय (devāya) – to the god; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; नमः (namaḥ) – salutation;
